summary.brief: Hampton v. Hearn. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: On March 8, 2011, Ms. Miles was treated by Dr. Hearn at Alamance Regional Medical Center for angioplasty and stent placement in her innominate vein related to her dialysis treatments.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Three days later, on March 11, 2011, Ms. Miles needed a 'permacath placement' in her right internal jugular vein to establish new access for her ongoing dialysis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During the procedure, Ms. Miles experienced ventricular tachycardia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Duke pathology report revealed that 'a foreign body' was found in the right ventricle. The foreign body was determined to be a 30 millimeter 'self-expanding stent which had a fracture on one portion of it.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Dahn concluded that Dr. Hearn’s placement of the stent breached the applicable standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Two expert witnesses retained by Dr. Hearn, Dr. Steve Powell and Dr. Ray Workman, testified that Dr. Hearn had complied with the standard of care when he performed the angioplasty and stent placement procedures.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's experts

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not err in instructing the jury on intervening negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff was not prejudiced by the admittance of Dr. Hearn’s expert witness testimony.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff alleged her disabling injuries to [Ms. Miles] as a result of injuries sustained when a stent improperly placed in [Ms. Miles’] vein for better dialysis access, was broken during a subsequent procedure and went into [Ms. Miles’] heart causing severe, permanent and disabling injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s instruction to the jury did not require Plaintiff to disprove superseding or intervening negligence by Onslow Urgent Care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Miles was treated for angioplasty and stent placement in her innominate vein related to her dialysis treatments.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Blocked innominate vein requiring angioplasty and stent placement
initial_differential: Vascular obstruction due to dialysis catheter
critical_findings: Stent placement required for dialysis access

requestables.name: Radiological identification of stent
requestables.description: Radiological imaging to confirm stent placement

reveal.result_summary: Stent placed at the junction of the left innominate vein and the superior vena cava
reveal.decision_impact: Ensures correct placement for dialysis access

requestables.name: Pathology report of foreign body
requestables.description: Analysis of foreign body found in right ventricle

reveal.result_summary: Foreign body identified as a fractured stent

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for angioplasty and stent placement

key_events.event: Stent placed extending into superior vena cava
Ventricular tachycardia and foreign body discovered

decision_points.clinical_context: Stent placement in innominate vein for dialysis access
decision_points.prompt: What is the appropriate placement for the stent?

options.description: Place stent extending minimally into the superior vena cava
options.clinical_reasoning: Minimizes risk of stent migration and fracture

options.description: Place stent extending significantly into the superior vena cava
options.clinical_reasoning: Increases risk of stent migration and fracture

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant placed stent extending significantly into the superior vena cava
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed it was acceptable practice

expected_action_court.description: Stent should have been placed minimally into the superior vena cava
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established minimal extension is standard

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Stent should have been placed with minimal extension
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced ventricular tachycardia and required removal of fractured stent

key_findings: Jury found Dr. Hearn not negligent

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper placement would have minimized risk

factual_timeline.event: Ventricular tachycardia and foreign body discovered
